title: Filing RERA Complaint Against Builder
description: Step-by-step guide to file a complaint with RERA Authority against a real estate developer/builder.

steps.details: Check if the project is registered with RERA on your state's RERA website. If the project is NOT registered and the builder is selling, that itself is a major violation. Note the RERA registration number.

Gather: sale agreement/allotment letter, payment receipts, builder-buyer agreement, correspondence with builder (emails, letters), brochure/advertisement showing promised amenities and delivery date, bank loan documents.

Before filing with RERA, send a written complaint to the builder via registered post/email. State your grievance clearly — delayed possession, structural defects, plan changes, etc. Give 15 days to respond.

Visit your state's RERA website and file the complaint online. Fill in: your details, project RERA number, builder details, nature of complaint, relief sought. Pay the prescribed fee (usually Rs. 1,000-5,000). Attach all supporting documents.
steps.timeline: 1 day to file; RERA should resolve within 60 days

steps.details: RERA Authority will schedule a hearing and send notice to the builder. You can appear in person or through a lawyer. Present your case with all evidence. The builder must respond.

RERA can order: refund with interest, possession with delay compensation, structural defect repair, or penalty on builder. If unsatisfied with the RERA order, appeal to RERA Appellate Tribunal within 60 days.
steps.timeline: Order within 60 days; Appeal within 60 days of order

estimated_timeline: RERA resolution: 60-90 days. Appeal: additional 60-90 days.
tips: You can file BOTH a RERA complaint and a consumer court complaint simultaneously
Interest on refund is usually SBI's highest lending rate + 2%
For structural defects, the builder MUST repair within 30 days (for 5 years after possession)
Group complaints with other buyers are stronger — coordinate with your flat owners' association
Always calculate the total amount (including interest) before accepting any settlement offer from the builder
If the builder's RERA registration is revoked, you still have rights under the Act
